title: Rounding rules must be explicit and consistent
description: Monetary rounding mode and the point at which it is applied are business decisions that change totals; applying them inconsistently creates penny discrepancies that fail reconciliation.
body: Use when splitting, taxing, or converting monetary amounts. The rounding mode (half-up vs half-even/banker's) and whether you round per line or only on the total materially change results, and allocating a rounded total across lines can leave leftover pennies that must be distributed deterministically. Do not let rounding vary by code path or default to language-specific float rounding. Actions: define one rounding mode and rounding point, allocate remainders deterministically, and verify sums of parts equal the rounded whole. Validation signals include tests that allocated parts re-sum to the total and reconciliation showing no rounding drift. Risk boundary: advisory only; a change to rounding rules affects real balances and must go through human review, never a silent default.
